questions.q: The Reserve Bank of India serves as the country's:
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the central bank of India.

questions.q: In which year was the Reserve Bank of India established?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India was established in 1935.

questions.q: In which year was the Reserve Bank of India nationalised?
questions.explanation: The RBI was nationalised in 1949, coming fully under government ownership.

questions.q: Which currency does the Reserve Bank of India issue?
questions.explanation: The RBI issues the Indian rupee, the national currency.

questions.q: One of the key responsibilities of the RBI is to formulate the country's:
questions.explanation: The RBI is responsible for formulating and implementing India's monetary policy.

questions.q: The RBI regulates which system of the country?
questions.explanation: The RBI regulates the banking and financial system of India.

questions.q: The RBI is often called the guardian of the:
questions.explanation: As issuer of currency and manager of monetary policy, the RBI is described as the guardian of the rupee.

questions.q: The head of the Reserve Bank of India is designated as the:
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India is headed by a Governor.

questions.q: The RBI acts as banker to which entity?
questions.explanation: The RBI acts as banker to the central and state governments.

questions.q: Where are the central offices of the Reserve Bank of India located?
questions.explanation: The central office of the RBI is located in Mumbai.

questions.q: The Reserve Bank of India was established under which Act?
options: The Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934
questions.explanation: The RBI was established under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, beginning operations in 1935.

questions.q: Who appoints the Governor of the Reserve Bank of India?
options: The President alone in personal discretion
questions.explanation: The Governor of the RBI is appointed by the Government of India (the central government).

questions.q: Who was the first Indian Governor of the Reserve Bank of India?
questions.explanation: C.D. Deshmukh was the first Indian to serve as Governor of the Reserve Bank of India.

questions.q: The RBI manages the country's reserves of:
questions.explanation: The RBI is the custodian and manager of India's foreign exchange reserves.

questions.q: The RBI acts as the lender of last resort to which institutions?
questions.explanation: The RBI serves as the lender of last resort to commercial banks during liquidity shortages.

questions.q: Currency notes in India, except the one-rupee note, are issued by the:
questions.explanation: All currency notes except the one-rupee note are issued by the RBI; the one-rupee note is issued by the Finance Ministry.

questions.q: The Governor of the RBI is assisted by a number of officials designated as:
questions.explanation: The Governor is assisted by Deputy Governors in running the Reserve Bank of India.

questions.q: The RBI's affairs are governed by a board of directors known as the:
questions.explanation: The RBI is governed by a Central Board of Directors appointed by the Government of India.

questions.q: The emblem of the Reserve Bank of India features a palm tree and which animal?
questions.explanation: The RBI's emblem depicts a palm tree and a tiger.

questions.q: The RBI uses tools such as the repo rate primarily to manage:
questions.explanation: The RBI uses instruments like the repo rate to regulate money supply and influence inflation.

questions.q: The RBI began functioning on 1 April of which year?
questions.explanation: Established under the 1934 Act, the RBI commenced operations on 1 April 1935.

questions.q: The RBI sells and manages government securities as part of its role in:
questions.explanation: The RBI manages the public debt of the government, including issuing and servicing government securities.

questions.q: Who was the first Governor of the Reserve Bank of India when it began operations in 1935?
questions.explanation: Sir Osborne Smith was the first Governor of the RBI when it commenced operations in 1935.

questions.q: The committee that sets the policy (repo) rate in India is the:
questions.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the policy repo rate.

questions.q: The Reserve Bank of India was set up partly on the recommendations of which earlier commission?
questions.explanation: The RBI was established on the recommendations of the Royal Commission on Indian Currency and Finance, the Hilton Young Commission.

questions.q: The RBI's original head office, before it shifted to Mumbai, was located in which city?
questions.explanation: The RBI's central office was originally in Calcutta (Kolkata) and was later moved to Bombay (Mumbai).

questions.q: The system under which the RBI issues currency notes against assets is known as the:
questions.explanation: Since 1957 the RBI has followed the Minimum Reserve System for issuing currency notes.

questions.q: Banks are required to keep a portion of their deposits with the RBI under the:
questions.explanation: Under the Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR), banks must hold a specified share of deposits with the RBI.

questions.q: The RBI was nationalised through the Reserve Bank (Transfer to Public Ownership) Act passed in which year?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank (Transfer to Public Ownership) Act was passed in 1948, bringing the RBI under public ownership from 1949.

questions.q: In addition to monetary policy, the RBI promotes the development of the financial system by regulating instruments such as:
questions.explanation: The RBI regulates and develops payment and settlement systems as part of its central banking functions.
